title.zh: Django Admin：调整 Save as new 的展示条件
title.en: Django Admin: adjust when Save as new is shown

summary.zh: 审查一个 Django admin PR：作者认为 Save as new 位于修改页，因此有 change 权限的用户也应该能看到这个动作。
summary.en: Review a Django admin PR: the author argues Save as new lives on the change page, so users with change permission should also see the action.

reviewTarget.note: Adapted from a real upstream issue for review training. Whether this change should be merged is the question — reach your own conclusion before opening the source links.

pr.title: Allow Save as new when a user can change the object
pr.author: codegen-agent
body: The admin change form currently hides Save as new unless the user has add permission, even though the button is rendered while editing an existing object. This is confusing for staff who can maintain existing records but do not have broad add access.

This PR treats Save as new as part of the change form action set: if the user can change the current object and save_as is enabled, the button is shown. The popup and change-form guards stay in place.
Added a submit_row regression test for a change-only user so the template context exposes show_save_as_new. Existing admin tests pass.

background: Django admin has separate add and change permissions. A user who can edit an existing object is not necessarily allowed to create a new object.
The Save as new control appears on a change form, but its result is a newly created object derived from the current one.
The review context includes the pre-patch submit_row() template-context logic in src-admin-modify.py.

notes: Save as new is a create operation, not just a change-form convenience. Removing has_add_permission from the display condition exposes a creation action to users who may only be authorized to edit existing objects.
This is an authorization boundary problem: UI visibility and the POST path must both reflect the action's semantics. A change-only user should neither see nor successfully perform Save as new.
The added test asserts the unsafe behavior. A useful regression test would assert that change-only users cannot see or submit Save as new, while users with both add and change permissions still can.

learningGoal: 识别 UI 按钮显示逻辑背后的动作语义和权限边界。

terms.description: 在 admin 中把当前对象另存为新对象的动作。
用户能执行的动作必须和权限模型一致。

behaviorChecks.expected: 可以编辑现有对象，但不能创建新对象。
behaviorChecks.reviewQuestion: Save as new 是否被错误展示或执行？

behaviorChecks.input: 同时有 add 和 change 权限
behaviorChecks.expected: 可以使用 Save as new。
服务端也应按创建动作检查 add 权限。
behaviorChecks.reviewQuestion: 补丁是否只改了 UI 条件？
